zh.lifeSummary: 當Joanna Webster於1552年出生在英國素福爾可易普斯維其時，她的父親John Webster III17歲，母親Alice Olven22歲。她於1573年與John Shatswell III在英國英格蘭素福爾可易普斯維其結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1653年4月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可易普斯維其享年101歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joanna Webster于1552年出生在英国素福爾可易普斯維其时，她的父亲John Webster III17岁，母亲Alice Olven22岁。她于1573年与John Shatswell III在英国英格兰素福爾可易普斯維其结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1653年4月17日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼艾可易普斯維其享年101岁。

nameMeanings.name: Webster
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a weaver, from Middle English webbester ‘weaver’ (Old English webbestre ‘female weaver’). By the time of surname formation, the gender distinction of the -stre suffix had almost completely disappeared. Compare Webb , Webber , and Weaver .

History: The name Webster was brought to North America from England independently by several different bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. One John Webster settled in Ipswich, MA, in 1635; another John Webster (died 1661), ancestor of the lexicographer Noah Webster, emigrated to Cambridge, MA, c. 1631 and later became one of the founders of the colony of CT, of which he was appointed governor in 1656. Daniel Webster (1782–1852), politician and orator, was born in Salisbury, NH, a descendant of Thomas Webster, a prominent 17th-century citizen of Ipswich, MA, whose family had settled there around 1635, while he was still a child.

variants.name: Webb
meanings: English: occupational name for a weaver, from early Middle English webbe (Old English webba (masculine) or webbe (feminine), probably used of both male and female weavers). This word survived into Middle English long enough to give rise to the surname, but was already obsolescent as an agent noun; hence the secondary forms with the agent suffixes -er and -ster (see Webster , Webber and compare Weaver ).
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, cognates of 1, including Weber and Weberman.
History: Richard Webb, a Lowland Scot, was an admitted freeman of Boston in 1632, and in 1635 was one of the first settlers of Hartford, CT.

nameMeanings.name: Joanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Latin form, Io(h)anna, of Greek Iōanna, the feminine equivalent of Iōannēs ( see John ). In the New Testament, this name is borne by a woman who was one of Jesus's followers (Luke 8:3; 24:10). She was the wife of the steward of the household of King Herod Antipas. The name was regularly used throughout the Middle Ages in most parts of Europe as a feminine equivalent of John , but in England it has only been in common use as a vernacular given name since the 19th century. Celebrated British bearers of the name include the novelist Joanna Trollope ( b. 1943 ), the actress Joanna Lumley ( b. 1946 ), and the concert pianist Joanna McGregor ( b. 1959 ).

variants.name: Joannah

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: When Joanna Webster was born in 1552, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, her father, John Webster III, was 17 and her mother, Alice Olven, was 22. She married John Shatswell III in 1573, in Ipswich, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 17 April 1653, in Ipswich, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 101.

ko.lifeSummary: Joanna Webster(이)가 1552년 영국 수폴크 이프스위치에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Webster III(은)는 17세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Olven(은)는 22세였다. 그녀는 1573년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 이프스위치에서 John Shatswell III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1653년 4월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 에세스 이프스위치에서 101세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joanna Webster nasceu em 1552, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Webster III, tinha 17 anos e sua mãe, Alice Olven, tinha 22 anos. Ela casou-se com John Shatswell III em 1573, em Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 17 de abril de 1653, em Ipswich, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 101 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joanna Webster родилась в 1552 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, её отцу по имени John Webster III было 17 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Olven, 22. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Shatswell III в 1573 г. в местности ипсвич, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла 17 апреля 1653 г. в местности ипсвич, эссекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 101 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joanna Webster nació en 1552, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, su papá, John Webster III, tenía 17 años y su mamá, Alice Olven, tenía 22 años. Se casó con John Shatswell III en 1573, en Ipswich, Suffolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 17 de abril de 1653, en Ipswich, Essex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 101 años.
